title: Matelea smithii
intro: Matelea smithii, also known as Smith's Swallowwort or Matelea, is a climbing vine native to the southeastern United States, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate pain and muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of Matelea smithii include alkaloids, specifically gerberifoline and gerberifoline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Matelea smithii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and menstrual cramps.
taxonomy: Matelea smithii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander.
